disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Explosive onset of widespread raised urticarial welts and severe skin burning/itch over roughly two hours after becoming hot
trigger_question_examples: What's happening with your skin?
When did this start?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: About two hours ago, after I got really hot, huge red welts blew up all over me and my skin is burning. New ones keep coming up.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Dermatographism — light stroking or a clothing seam produces a new linear wheal within a minute
trigger_question_examples: Does anything touching your skin make it worse?
If you scratch clear skin, what happens?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes — if I scratch normal skin or a clothing seam presses on me, a raised red line comes up right there within a minute.

trigger_question_examples: Do you look flushed or sweaty?
What does your face and chest look like?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My face and chest are really red and flushed and I've broken out in a sweat.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No throat tightness and no difficulty swallowing — voice sounds normal to the patient
trigger_question_examples: Does your throat feel tight or like it's closing?
Any trouble swallowing, or does your voice sound different?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No — my throat feels normal, I can swallow fine and my voice sounds the same as always.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No shortness of breath, no wheeze — able to speak in full sentences
trigger_question_examples: Are you short of breath or wheezing?
Can you speak a full sentence without stopping for breath?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I'm breathing OK — I'm not wheezing and I can talk in whole sentences without gasping.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No dizziness or lightheadedness on standing and no faintness — no sense of impending collapse
trigger_question_examples: Do you feel faint or dizzy, especially when you stand up?
Do you feel like you might pass out?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I don't feel faint or dizzy — I'm up and pacing around, I just can't stop scratching.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No vomiting, abdominal cramps or diarrhoea
trigger_question_examples: Any nausea, vomiting, tummy cramps or diarrhoea?
Is your stomach upset at all?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, my stomach's fine — no vomiting or cramps.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Took one 10 mg over-the-counter antihistamine an hour ago with no effect
trigger_question_examples: Have you taken anything for it?
Did any medication help?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I took one of my over-the-counter antihistamine tablets about an hour ago and it did nothing at all.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Known background mast-cell / histamine condition under specialist investigation, with prior similar but milder skin flares
trigger_question_examples: Have you had anything like this before?
Do you have any ongoing medical conditions?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've got a mast cell / histamine problem a specialist has been looking into — I get skin flares from it, but nothing this big before.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Patient lives alone and is highly anxious about the reaction spreading internally and closing the airway
trigger_question_examples: Is anyone with you?
What's worrying you most right now?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I'm home on my own and I'm terrified this is going to spread inside and I won't be able to breathe.
